messages.author: Brock
messages.text: Repeaters beyond Kamloops that are heard in Kamloops.
messages.content_html: Repeaters beyond Kamloops that are heard in Kamloops.

messages.author: Brock
messages.text: TX delay parameter seems to be improved connectivity. Will have to keep watching.
messages.content_html: TX delay parameter seems to be improved connectivity. Will have to keep watching.

messages.author: ka.RFz
messages.text: Nice, are those repeaters to the east of Lolo the ones Dan has been setting up?
messages.content_html: Nice, are those repeaters to the east of Lolo the ones Dan has been setting up?

messages.author: Brock
messages.text: Pretty much every mountaintop repeater is Dan's doing. I think Silver Star repeaters are Michael's, but I haven't actually asked the question!
messages.content_html: Pretty much every mountaintop repeater is Dan's doing. I think Silver Star repeaters are Michael's, but I haven't actually asked the question!
messages.reply_html:
ka.RFz
Nice, are those repeaters to the east of Lolo the ones Dan has been setting up?

messages.author: ka.RFz
messages.text: Gotcha. Yeah I ran into Dan on another platform and thought some of the issues you were working through sounded familiar.
messages.content_html: Gotcha. Yeah I ran into Dan on another platform and thought some of the issues you were working through sounded familiar.

messages.author: Brock
messages.text: He has MT in all of them too. He seemed to have better results by playing with the AGC Reset parameter. I suspect that both the delay and reset tweaks were important. I still haven't figured out why the AUTOMATIC Gain Control needs to be reset. That seems to contradict the purpose of the function...
messages.content_html: He has MT in all of them too. He seemed to have better results by playing with the AGC Reset parameter. I suspect that both the delay and reset tweaks were important. I still haven't figured out why the AUTOMATIC Gain Control needs to be reset. That seems to contradict the purpose of the function...

messages.author: sNEAKYnIGHTmUPPET
messages.text: I think I read somewhere that AGC averages over time. so if something is yelling in its ear constantly, then natural decay may take too long.
messages.content_html: I think I read somewhere that AGC averages over time. so if something is yelling in its ear constantly, then natural decay may take too long.

messages.author: Brock
messages.text: So it's a really long delay essentially... A strange implementation of AGC.
messages.content_html: So it's a really long delay essentially... A strange implementation of AGC.

messages.author: sNEAKYnIGHTmUPPET
messages.text: I am speculating here, I have not looked into the data sheets
messages.content_html: I am speculating here, I have not looked into the data sheets

messages.author: Brock
messages.text: Well, it would certainly fit the symptoms, wouldn't it?
messages.content_html: Well, it would certainly fit the symptoms, wouldn't it?

messages.author: Brock
messages.text: Was up on Greenstone Mountain yesterday. My T1000 was able to hit Lolo and Paxton repeaters with big SNR numbers. Terrace Mountain was in the positive, but only just. A better antenna set up a few meters would probably improve all those SNR numbers.
messages.content_html: Was up on Greenstone Mountain yesterday. My T1000 was able to hit Lolo and Paxton repeaters with big SNR numbers. Terrace Mountain was in the positive, but only just. A better antenna set up a few meters would probably improve all those SNR numbers.

messages.author: sNEAKYnIGHTmUPPET
messages.text: I am sometimes shocked how well the t1000 actually works with its little internal antenna
messages.content_html: I am sometimes shocked how well the t1000 actually works with its little internal antenna
